text:
Once upon a time, in the heart of a lush, verdant forest, there was a small but magical lake. The water of this lake was said to have the power to grant wishes, but only to those who were pure of heart and truly needed it. The lake was surrounded by a dense fog that kept it hidden from most, making it a secret known only to a few.

Among those who knew about the lake was a young girl named Lila. Lila was kind-hearted and always helped those around her, whether they were her friends or strangers. One day, she encountered an old man who had lost his way in the woods. His eyes were weary, and his clothes were tattered. Lila took pity on him and offered to guide him back to the village.

As they walked, Lila shared stories about the magical lake, and the old man's interest piqued. He asked if he could see it for himself. Lila hesitated; the lake was sacred
